description:
You are a Farmworker responsible for cattle health records at a commercial beef operation. Document a bovine respiratory disease (BRD) outbreak in Pen 12 by creating five logs in the farmOS Field Kit app for regulatory compliance and veterinary records.

Create a 'Done' Observation log named 'Pen 12 BRD respiratory assessment' at 7:00 AM with notes: '12 of 45 head showing BRD signs: coughing, nasal discharge, elevated respiration rate. Rectal temps 103-105F. Pen pull list prepared for treatment. Healthy animals appear normal.'

Create a 'Done' Input log named 'Enrofloxacin BRD treatment 12 head' at 9:00 AM with notes: 'Enrofloxacin 7.5 mg/kg SQ injection. 12 animals treated individually. 28-day beef withdrawal period. Product lot EX2024-J, exp 06/2025. Weight-based dosing verified by headcount.'

Create a 'Done' Activity log named 'Sick pen setup and animal movement' at 9:45 AM with notes: 'Moved 12 treated animals to sick pen east barn. Fresh bedding added, water access doubled, hay bunk positioned for easy monitoring. Tagged all 12 with orange sick pen tags.'

Create a 'NOT Done' Observation log named '48hr BRD treatment response check' at 7:00 AM with notes: '8 of 12 animals improving: eating, reduced respiratory effort, temps 102-103F. 4 animals remain symptomatic with persistent nasal discharge and labored breathing. Veterinarian assessment scheduled for tomorrow.'

Create a 'NOT Done' Input log named 'Non-responder vet exam and hold' at 9:00 AM with notes: '4 non-responding animals isolated for veterinary examination. Do not treat further until vet assessment completed. Tulathromycin protocol pending vet approval. Withdrawal records to be updated after treatment decision.'

metadata.role: Farmworker, Farm, Ranch, and Aquacultural Animals

required_logs.name: Sick pen setup and animal movement
